source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure below, two concentric conducting spherical shells, centered at \( r = 0 \) and having radii \( r = c \) and \( r = d \), are maintained at potentials such that the potential \( V(r) \) at \( r = c \) is \( V_1 \) and \( V(r) \) at \( r = d \) is \( V_2 \). Assume that \( V(r) \) depends only on \( r \), where \( r \) is the radial distance. The expression for \( V(r) \) in the region between \( r = c \) and \( r = d \) is
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: \( V(r) = \frac{cd(V_1 - V_2)}{(d - c)r} - \frac{V_2 d - V_1 c}{d - c} \)

choice_map.B: \( V(r) = \frac{cd(V_1 - V_2)}{(d - c)r} - \frac{V_2 d - V_1 c}{d - c} \)
choice_map.C: \( V(r) = \frac{cd(V_1 - V_2)}{(d - c)r} - \frac{V_1 c - V_2 c}{d - c} \)
choice_map.D: \( V(r) = \frac{cd(V_2 - V_1)}{(d - c)r} - \frac{V_2 c - V_1 c}{d - c} \)
